name: Information Security Policy
description: Defines the overall approach to managing information security risks and establishes the security objectives for the organization.

name: Access Control Policy
description: Governs how access to systems, networks, and data is provisioned, reviewed, and revoked for employees and third parties.

name: Incident Response Policy
description: Outlines the procedures for detecting, reporting, investigating, containing, and recovering from security incidents.

name: Vulnerability Management Policy
description: Defines the process for identifying, classifying, prioritizing, and remediating security vulnerabilities across systems and software.

name: Change Management Policy
description: Establishes the process for requesting, reviewing, approving, and implementing changes to production systems and infrastructure.

name: Data Classification and Handling Policy
description: Defines how data is classified by sensitivity level and specifies the required handling, storage, transmission, and disposal procedures for each classification.

name: Vendor Risk Management Policy
description: Describes how the organization identifies, assesses, and manages risks associated with third-party vendors and service providers.

name: Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery Policy
description: Outlines the strategy and procedures for maintaining business continuity and recovering IT systems following a disruptive event.

name: Acceptable Use Policy
description: Defines the acceptable use of company systems, networks, and data by employees, contractors, and other authorized users.

name: Cryptography and Encryption Policy
description: Governs the use of cryptographic controls, including encryption standards, key management, and certificate lifecycle management.
